userStories.title: Cache lexsort of regime means to avoid O(n log n) per bar
userStories.description: HMMEngine._label_regimes already computes np.lexsort over regime means to order them by return. predict_filtered then recomputes the same lexsort on every bar to find the rank for the currently-detected regime. Since regime means are fixed between retrains, cache sorted ids and an id→rank lookup at the end of _label_regimes and read from the cache in predict_filtered. Reset/rebuild the cache when the model changes (train/load).
acceptanceCriteria: HMMEngine has self._sorted_regime_ids: np.ndarray assigned at the tail of _label_regimes()
HMMEngine has self._regime_id_to_rank: dict[int, int] giving O(1) rank lookup
predict_filtered uses the cached _regime_id_to_rank for label resolution; no np.lexsort call in predict_filtered
load() rebuilds the cache from regime_infos (call _label_regimes again OR derive cache directly from restored model means)
reset_inference_state does NOT clear the sort cache (it is model-state, not inference-state)
A new regression test asserts predict_filtered gives the same label before and after hot-path refactor by comparing against the expected mapping from _label_regimes output
tests/hmm still has 47+ tests passing after refactor

userStories.title: Retire legacy StrategySignal dataclass
userStories.description: StrategySignal was kept for backward compatibility during the c003 migration to spec Signal. A grep confirms no production code emits or consumes it. Remove the class, remove it from __all__ and __init__.py exports, and confirm the codebase still passes.
acceptanceCriteria: grep for 'StrategySignal' in shared/ returns zero matches
grep for 'StrategySignal' in tests/ returns zero matches
shared/core/hmm/__init__.py does not import or export StrategySignal
uv run python -m pytest tests/hmm -q passes
uv run ruff check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm passes with no unused-import errors

userStories.title: Full quality gate: ruff + ty + pytest green after refactors
userStories.description: Regression checks after HMM-FU-001 and HMM-FU-002. Ensures nothing else regresses.
acceptanceCriteria: uv run ruff check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm exits 0
uv run ruff format --check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm exits 0
uv run ty check shared/core/hmm tests/hmm reports no in-scope errors
uv run python -m pytest tests/hmm -q shows all tests passing
